blockers: Managed default gateway service argv is not explicitly profile-bound and can follow sticky active_profile.
Routine supervised starts use broad --replace authority that can take over credential locks across Hermes homes.
Independent profile listeners have no topology preflight or durable host-level port ownership contract.
Shared Kanban dispatcher ownership is a startup race rather than an explicit per-board profile assignment.
Hermes does not expose the exact identity, stale-state precondition, receipt, and readback contracts Cabinet needs for governed live lifecycle actions.
recommendation: Upstream explicit service profile identity and same-profile-only replacement first, then add listener leases and explicit per-board dispatcher ownership before Cabinet implements live lifecycle controls.
root_cause: Default is encoded by an omitted profile flag even though omission also enables sticky active-profile resolution; supervisor recovery then combines the resulting ambiguous identity with broad replacement authority.

behaviors: explicit default and named service identity
port collision preflight and lease keys

source_changes_required: Always emit --profile default in generated managed service argv.
Replace broad routine --replace with exact same-profile replacement.
Persist and verify profile, canonical home, service, PID start time, and generation.
Add named-profile explicit-port validation, topology preflight, and machine-global listener leases.
Make Agent API ownership visible as a profile/platform listener resource.
Assign shared dispatcher ownership explicitly per board.
migration: Require explicit named-profile ports while preserving default compatibility.
Assign legacy boards to default dispatcher owner with governed drain for a live non-default owner.
Restart and verify one exact profile generation at a time.
risks: Supervisor process matching must be updated for explicit default.
Strict port validation can break implicit legacy configurations.
IPv4 and IPv6 wildcard overlap needs platform-specific tests.
Credential ownership locks must remain machine-global.
Dispatcher migration may move active work.
